model_name: LOKX capture-separated landmark-calibrated multimode PSL-inspired heuristic
selection_rule: The user selects masculine, feminine or neutral/editorial before camera or upload. Sex and gender are never inferred.

calibration_anchors.note: Internal alignment percentages are heuristic scores, not percentiles. Piecewise anchors are tuned to the selected community tier boundaries and remain experimental.

mesh_coordinate_calibration.alignment_compression: raw ideal-band alignment is compressed to 25 + raw*0.5 before mode weighting, preventing a single ratio from becoming either 0 or 100
mesh_coordinate_calibration.note: Bands are defined in MediaPipe mesh coordinates. They are not interchangeable with caliper anthropometry or classical hairline-based facial thirds.

feminine.normalization: The low-confidence pixel proxy is used only when available. Otherwise its weight is excluded and measured weights are normalized.

neutral_editorial.normalization: The facial luminance contrast proxy is used only when pixels are available. Styling remains unmeasured.

penalty_rule.score: mode_weighted_score converted through community-balanced piecewise calibration, minus reduced explicit adjustments, then limited only by broad upper-tier consistency gates
penalty_rule.capture_penalty: Only a consented single-photo analysis may receive a small image-condition adjustment. Guided camera and shared-window scans use capture quality only for confidence, uncertainty and proxy availability.
additional_rules: weakest-link and component-spread adjustments are reduced to avoid double counting
live capture quality never removes PSL

tier_gates.capture_confidence_rule: Below 68 widens the estimate and adds a reliability warning; it does not demote the face by itself.

tier_ranges.shared: Sub-3 / Very Low Tier; dehumanizing forum labels omitted

measurement_limits: Percent scores are internal heuristic alignment, not population percentiles.
Landmark bands are calibrated to MediaPipe canonical-mesh coordinates; classical anthropometric ratios cannot be pasted directly onto different landmark pairs.
Guided live capture quality never removes PSL; it only changes confidence, uncertainty and whether pixel/depth proxies are usable.
Chin projection, deep-set eyes, hunter eyes, jaw structure and cheekbones are labeled as 2D visual proxies where appropriate.
Facial contrast uses adapted Michelson luminance contrast and CIELAB differences across eyes, eyebrows and mouth versus surrounding facial skin.
Skin-surface uniformity and facial contrast remain capture-dependent proxies affected by lighting, makeup, camera and compression; they do not measure skin health.
Hair occlusion, beard occlusion, body fat, makeup, styling and facial contrast are not guessed from landmarks.
Gonial, ramus and orbital outputs are explicitly surface/depth proxies from the MediaPipe model, never anatomical or metric measurements.
Photographic cervicomental angle is attempted only from a dedicated side profile using the SelfieMulticlass face-skin/body-skin boundary and is rejected below the confidence threshold.
The photographic neck proxy is not cephalometry, anatomy, diagnosis or a metric 3D measurement and may fail with beard, hair, collars, accessories, occlusion or poor lighting.
Webcam RGB depth is relative and non-metric; metric 3D requires calibrated stereo, structured light, LiDAR or a validated 3D scanner.
No output predicts identity, personality, health, ancestry or universal attractiveness.
